title: Pipeline Builder - Overview - Palantir
summary: Pipeline Builder is Foundry's primary application for data integration, enabling users to build data integration pipelines that transform raw data sources into clean outputs. It leverages both Spark and Flink, uses a next-generation data transformation backend, and supports batch, incremental, and streaming pipeline types through a point-and-click interface.
relevantParts: Pipeline Builder architecture using Spark and Flink compute engines
Supports batch, incremental, and streaming pipeline types
Compute-agnostic Build framework with integrated security and data lineage

title: Overview - Data Integration - Palantir
summary: Foundry provides a highly configurable set of data connectivity and integration tools that extend far beyond typical ETL/ELT solutions. The extensible data connection framework establishes connections with all types of source systems (structured, unstructured, semi-structured) using batch, micro-batch, or streaming transfer approaches. Designed to reduce the cost of data integration over time.
relevantParts: Comprehensive overview of Foundry's data integration architecture
Extensible framework for all source system types
Pipeline Builder as the primary data integration application

title: What is a data pipeline? - Palantir
summary: Explains the concept of a data pipeline in Foundry as the flow of data from a source system through intermediate datasets to produce high-quality curated datasets. These curated datasets can be structured into the Ontology or serve as the foundation for machine learning and analytical workflows.
relevantParts: Defines the end-to-end data pipeline concept in Foundry
Shows how pipelines connect raw data to Ontology and analytics

title: Pipeline Builder - Core Concepts - Palantir
summary: Covers Pipeline Builder's core concepts including graph and form-based interfaces, type-safe functions that flag errors immediately, strict output checks to prevent unintentional downstream breaks, and version control via pipeline branches similar to Git code branches.
relevantParts: Graph-based pipeline editing interface details
Type-safe functions and strict output checks for data quality
Pipeline branching for version control

title: Pipeline Builder - Transforms - Overview - Palantir
summary: Documents the transforms available within Pipeline Builder, enabling users to apply data transformations without specialized programming language knowledge. Pipeline Builder integrates various programming languages beneath a streamlined builder interface.
relevantParts: Available transforms in Pipeline Builder
No-code data transformation capabilities

title: Data Connection - Overview - Palantir
summary: Data Connection is the application used to synchronize data from external systems into Foundry. It supports batch, streaming, media, and CDC syncs, virtual tables, and manages associated resources including source credentials. Designed to make it simple to explore data sources and sync new data for use case development.
relevantParts: Central hub for all external data synchronization
Supports batch, streaming, media, CDC sync modes
Virtual tables for querying without data movement

title: Data Connection - Core Concepts - Palantir
summary: Explains Data Connection's core concepts including sources, connectors (source types), syncs, and the relationship between them. A source represents a connection to an external system, set up based on a particular connector, with a broad range of connectors available to support common enterprise data systems.
relevantParts: Defines sources, connectors, and sync primitives
Explains connector-based architecture for extensibility

title: Data Connection - Sources - Set up a source - Palantir
summary: Step-by-step guide for setting up a data source in Foundry, covering network configuration, credentials management via dedicated service accounts, and source creation through the Data Connection interface. Explains the difference between Foundry worker and agent worker connection types.
relevantParts: Practical guide for configuring external data sources
Network and credential management details
Worker types: Foundry worker vs agent worker

title: Data Connection - Sources - Source Exploration - Palantir
summary: Documents the source exploration feature in Data Connection that allows users to browse and discover available data within connected external systems before setting up syncs.
relevantParts: Source exploration capabilities for data discovery

title: On dataset versioning in Palantir Foundry - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir blog post explaining dataset versioning in Foundry. Datasets use immutable versions (like Git commits) enabling concurrent access. Four transaction types exist: SNAPSHOT, APPEND, UPDATE, and DELETE. The system combines dataset branching with Git branching to support versioning over both logic and data simultaneously.
relevantParts: Immutable dataset versions with Git-like semantics
Four transaction types: SNAPSHOT, APPEND, UPDATE, DELETE
Concurrent access safety through dataset branching

title: Core Concepts - Datasets - Palantir
summary: Defines the dataset as the most essential representation of data in Foundry. A dataset is fundamentally a wrapper around a collection of files stored in a backing file system. Datasets are updated over time using transactions which represent modifications to files within a dataset, with each version being immutable.
relevantParts: Foundational definition of datasets in Foundry
Transaction-based update model
Dataset views and historical versioning

title: Core Concepts - Branching - Palantir
summary: Explains dataset branching in Foundry, which provides foundational semantics for versioning data. Foundry combines dataset branching with Git branching to support branching over logic and data simultaneously, allowing different users to write concurrently to their own branches.
relevantParts: Dataset branching for parallel data development
Integration of dataset and code branching

title: Why Data Pipeline Version Control Matters - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir blog post covering data pipeline version control, explaining why tracking code and data together is essential. Versioned output datasets enable safely scheduling transformation jobs across the data platform without risk of data corruption.
relevantParts: Rationale for combined code+data version control
Safe scheduling of transformation jobs with versioned outputs

title: Palantir Foundry's dataset version control, a diff-based Git for data - Hacker News
summary: Hacker News discussion about Foundry's dataset version control system, comparing it to a diff-based Git for data. Community discussion of the technical approach and its tradeoffs versus other data versioning systems.
relevantParts: Community analysis of Foundry's Git-like data versioning
Comparison with other data versioning approaches

title: Ontology SDK - Overview - Palantir
summary: The Ontology Software Development Kit (OSDK) provides access to the full power of the Ontology from external development environments. Supports TypeScript (NPM), Python (Pip/Conda), Java (Maven), and OpenAPI spec. Enables reading and writing to the Ontology with minimal code, treating Foundry as a backend.
relevantParts: Multi-language SDK for external Ontology access
Programmatic read/write to Ontology objects
Token-scoped security model for data access

title: palantir/osdk-ts - TypeScript OSDK Libraries - GitHub
summary: Official GitHub repository for the Palantir TypeScript OSDK libraries. Contains the open-source implementation of the Ontology SDK for TypeScript/JavaScript environments, enabling developers to interact with Foundry's Ontology programmatically.
relevantParts: Open-source TypeScript SDK implementation
Source code for Ontology integration patterns

title: Ontology SDK - Python OSDK - Palantir
summary: Documentation for the Python OSDK enabling Python developers to access Ontology objects, perform filtering, aggregations, and execute actions. Provides programmatic access to the full Ontology data model from Python environments.
relevantParts: Python SDK for Ontology data access
Filtering, aggregation, and action execution capabilities

title: Data Lineage - Overview - Palantir
summary: Data Lineage is an interactive tool providing a holistic view of how data flows through the Foundry platform. It presents the graph of datasets and transforms to show inputs and outputs, enables managing builds and schedules, and tracks downstream usage including Workshop Apps and Ontology Objects.
relevantParts: Visual graph of dataset and transform relationships
Build management and scheduling from lineage view
Tracks downstream Ontology and application usage

title: Data Lineage - Graphs - Explore data lineage - Palantir
summary: Details how to explore data pipelines using the Data Lineage graph interface, including searching for resources (datasets, object types), adding them to the graph, and visualizing upstream/downstream dependencies.
relevantParts: Interactive lineage graph exploration
Resource search and graph visualization

title: Data Lineage - Graphs - Explore artifacts and ontology entities - Palantir
summary: Documents how Data Lineage tracks not just datasets but also Ontology entities, showing the connection between raw data artifacts and their downstream Ontology representations.
relevantParts: Lineage tracking for Ontology entities alongside datasets
Connection between data artifacts and Ontology objects

title: Introduction to Data Lineage - Palantir Learn
summary: Palantir Learn course introducing Data Lineage concepts, providing an educational walkthrough of how data lineage works within the Foundry platform and how to use it effectively.
relevantParts: Educational content on data lineage usage

title: Code Repositories - Transforms - Create Transforms - Palantir
summary: Documents how to create transforms in Code Repositories using Python, Java, or SQL. Transforms are pieces of logic that take one or more inputs and return a single output (Foundry datasets or models). Supports lightweight transforms for smaller datasets and Spark transforms for distributed computing.
relevantParts: Multi-language transform creation (Python, Java, SQL)
Lightweight vs Spark transforms based on data size
Code-based pipeline development workflow

title: Python - Basic transforms - Transforms - Palantir
summary: Documentation for Python-based data transforms in Foundry, covering the foundry_transforms library for defining input/output relationships and transformation logic. Supports pandas, Polars, and Spark compute engines.
relevantParts: Python transform API and syntax
Multiple compute engines: pandas, Polars, Spark

title: Data Connection - External Transforms - Palantir
summary: External transforms allow connections to external systems from Python transforms repositories. Used primarily for batch sync, export, and media sync workflows when existing Data Connection source types are unavailable or lack desired features.
relevantParts: Custom Python-based external system integration
Fallback when standard connectors are insufficient

title: Code Examples - Incremental Transforms - Palantir
summary: Provides code examples for implementing incremental transforms in Foundry, showing how to process only new data that has changed since the last pipeline run rather than recomputing entire datasets.
relevantParts: Practical code examples for incremental processing
Patterns for efficient data pipeline updates

title: Overview - Ontology - Palantir
summary: The Foundry Ontology is an operational layer sitting on top of digital assets integrated into Foundry, connecting them to real-world counterparts. Defines semantics by mapping existing datasources into objects, properties, and links. The Object Data Funnel orchestrates data writes from Foundry datasources and user edits into object databases.
relevantParts: How datasources are mapped to Ontology objects, properties, links
Object Data Funnel microservice for data indexing
Ontology as the bridge between raw data and applications

title: Overview and getting started - Object Backend Overview - Palantir
summary: Documents the Object Storage V2 architecture, the next-generation canonical data store for the Ontology. OSv2 separates subsystems for indexing and querying data, enabling horizontal scaling. The Object Data Funnel reads data from Foundry datasources and user edits and indexes them into object databases.
relevantParts: Object Storage V2 architecture details
Separation of indexing and querying subsystems
Object Data Funnel pipeline orchestration

title: Palantir Foundry: Ontology - Medium
summary: Medium article explaining how raw datasets (databases, logs, APIs, CSVs) are brought into Foundry and mapped to Object Types using the Ontology Manager or SDKs. Covers the process of defining object types with attributes and relationships to transform raw data into a unified knowledge system.
relevantParts: End-to-end walkthrough from raw data to Ontology objects
Ontology Manager and SDK-based mapping approaches

title: Foundational Ontologies in Palantir Foundry - Medium
summary: Medium article exploring foundational ontology design patterns in Palantir Foundry, discussing best practices for structuring ontology object types and relationships for enterprise use cases.
relevantParts: Ontology design patterns and best practices
Foundational structuring of enterprise ontologies

title: Understanding the Palantir Ontology - PVM Blog
summary: Third-party analysis of the Palantir Ontology concept, explaining how it bridges the gap between raw data and business-meaningful entities to power real-world applications and decision-making.
relevantParts: External perspective on Ontology value proposition
How Ontology enables operational applications

title: Introduction - API Reference - Palantir
summary: Introduction to the Foundry REST API, a collection of HTTP endpoints using OAuth 2.0 authentication with JSON requests and responses. Covers how to find hostnames, authenticate, and make requests. Platform SDKs available for supported languages to simplify API calls.
relevantParts: REST API architecture and authentication patterns
OAuth 2.0 protocol for API access
Foundation for programmatic data integration

title: Available Connectors - REST APIs - Palantir
summary: Documents the REST API source connector in Data Connection for integrating with external systems that expose REST APIs. Supports syncing data to Foundry by making a series of REST API calls, with both Direct Connection and Agent-Based Source options.
relevantParts: REST API connector for ingesting from external REST services
Direct Connection vs Agent-Based Source architectures

title: REST Data Connections in Palantir Foundry - Medium
summary: Medium article with practical guidance on configuring REST data connections in Palantir Foundry, including OAuth Client Credentials flow and examples of connecting to various REST API sources.
relevantParts: Practical REST API connection configuration examples
OAuth Client Credentials flow for REST sources

title: Data Protection and Governance - Palantir
summary: Covers data governance as the management of enterprise data across the entire lifecycle from ingestion to access to deletion. Data protection and governance are central to Foundry, with markings implementing mandatory access controls and Sensitive Data Scanner detecting sensitive information automatically.
relevantParts: Data governance lifecycle from ingestion to deletion
Markings as mandatory access controls on data
Sensitive Data Scanner for automated detection

title: Platform Overview - Architecture - Palantir
summary: Overview of Foundry's microservices architecture. AIP and Foundry consist of 300+ microservices and assets running in a highly available, autoscaling compute mesh atop zero-trust security infrastructure. Covers the platform's core architectural principles.
relevantParts: 300+ microservices architecture overview
Zero-trust security infrastructure
Autoscaling compute mesh

title: The Technical Anatomy of Palantir's Foundry Platform: An In-Depth Analysis - Medium
summary: In-depth technical analysis of Palantir Foundry's platform architecture by a third-party author, covering the system's core components, data processing capabilities, and how various services interact.
relevantParts: Independent technical analysis of Foundry architecture
System component interactions and data flow

title: Data Connection - Architecture - Palantir
summary: Documents the Data Connection architecture including Foundry worker with direct connection policies, Foundry worker with agent proxy policies, and agent worker connections. Covers credential encryption using AES-256-GCM and agent-based networking for private network access.
relevantParts: Three architecture types for data connection
Credential security with AES-256-GCM encryption
Agent proxy for private network connectivity

title: Core Concepts - Streams - Palantir
summary: Explains Foundry streams as a data representation with both a persistent hot buffer and cold storage. Streams provide the same primitives as datasets (branching, version control, permissions, schema management) while also offering low-latency data access. Data is archived from hot to cold storage every few minutes.
relevantParts: Stream architecture with hot buffer and cold storage
Same governance primitives as datasets but with low latency
Archiving process from hot to cold storage

title: Building Pipelines - Streaming Pipelines - Overview - Palantir
summary: Streaming pipelines run continuously and process new data as it arrives, enabling immediate critical decisions based on real-time data. On average, streaming data can be accessible in the Ontology and available for analysis in under 15 seconds.
relevantParts: Sub-15-second latency for Ontology data availability
Continuous processing for real-time decision-making

title: Data Connection - Push data into a stream - Palantir
summary: Documents push-based ingestion for Foundry streams, supporting event-based workflows where external systems push data directly into Foundry rather than Foundry pulling data on a schedule.
relevantParts: Push-based ingestion model for event-driven data
Alternative to pull-based sync patterns

title: Contour - Overview - Palantir
summary: Contour provides a point-and-click interface for data analysis on tables at scale, creating interactive dashboards for guided exploration. Analysis flows through boards in series, starting with a dataset and applying various transformations and visualizations.
relevantParts: Interactive data analysis and visualization tool
Board-based analysis workflow on datasets

title: Core Concepts - Change Data Capture (CDC) - Palantir
summary: Foundry supports syncing, processing, and storing data from CDC feeds. CDC is used to stream real-time updates from relational databases, leveraging Debezium. Components throughout the platform (connectors, streams, pipelines, Ontology) all work natively with changelog data. Supports before/after change tracking for audit trails and SCD Type 2.
relevantParts: CDC integration using Debezium for database log reading
Native changelog support across connectors, streams, pipelines, Ontology
Before/after change tracking for audit trails

title: Core Concepts - Virtual Tables - Palantir
summary: Virtual tables act as pointers to tables in source systems outside Foundry, abstracting away the underlying source system and storage formats. They can be combined with Foundry datasets, support BigQuery, Databricks, Snowflake, and open table formats. Update detection monitors changes in source systems.
relevantParts: Query external data without moving it into Foundry
Supports BigQuery, Databricks, Snowflake, cloud storage
Update detection for triggering dependent pipelines

title: Core Concepts - Iceberg Tables - Palantir
summary: Apache Iceberg is available in Foundry as both managed and virtual Iceberg tables. Foundry natively implements the Iceberg REST Catalog specification and supports third-party catalogs like Databricks Unity Catalog. Benefits include interoperability, automated compaction, DELETE/UPDATE/MERGE INTO support, and incremental changelog consumption.
relevantParts: Native Iceberg REST Catalog implementation
Managed vs virtual Iceberg table types
Open format interoperability with third-party catalogs

title: Health Checks - Overview - Palantir
summary: Data Health is a Foundry service that monitors and alerts on common issues across datasets. Monitors for issues related to dataset status, time, size, content, and schema using customizable checks. Integrates with Foundry Notifications, emails, PagerDuty, Slack, and arbitrary REST webhooks for alerting.
relevantParts: Automated data quality monitoring for pipeline health
Multi-channel alerting (PagerDuty, Slack, webhooks)
Check types: status, time, size, content, schema

title: Building Pipelines - Types of Pipelines - Palantir
summary: Describes the three primary pipeline types in Foundry: batch pipelines (fully recompute on each run), incremental pipelines (process only new/changed data), and streaming pipelines (run continuously). Each type has different latency, cost, and use case characteristics.
relevantParts: Clear comparison of batch, incremental, and streaming pipeline types
Tradeoffs between latency, cost, and completeness

title: Building Pipelines - Incremental Pipelines - Overview - Palantir
summary: Incremental pipelines process only new data that has changed since the last run, avoiding unnecessary computation on unchanged rows/files. Enables lower end-to-end latency (on the order of minutes) while minimizing compute costs. Requires input changes through APPEND or UPDATE transactions.
relevantParts: Incremental processing for efficiency and lower latency
Transaction-type requirements for incremental operation
Minute-level latency achievable

title: A Practical Guide to Using the @incremental Decorator in Foundry - Medium
summary: Third-party practical guide explaining how to use the @incremental decorator for Python transforms in Foundry, covering common patterns and pitfalls for building efficient incremental data pipelines.
relevantParts: Practical Python code patterns for incremental transforms
Common pitfalls and best practices

title: Indexing - Overview - Palantir
summary: Documents the object indexing system, where the Object Data Funnel microservice orchestrates data writes into the Ontology via OSv2. Covers funnel batch and streaming pipelines, hydration of search nodes, and incremental object indexing for improved performance.
relevantParts: Object Data Funnel architecture for Ontology writes
Funnel batch vs streaming pipelines
Incremental object indexing and hydration process

title: Indexing - Funnel Batch Pipelines - Palantir
summary: Details how funnel batch pipelines orchestrate efficient indexing of data from Foundry datasources and user edits into OSv2. Covers live pipelines for production updates and replacement pipelines for schema changes requiring full reindexing.
relevantParts: Live vs replacement funnel pipeline types
Production data update orchestration
Schema change handling via replacement pipelines

title: Indexing - Funnel Streaming Pipelines - Palantir
summary: Documents funnel streaming pipelines for real-time indexing of streaming data into the Ontology via OSv2, enabling sub-15-second Ontology availability for streaming data sources.
relevantParts: Real-time streaming data indexing into Ontology
Low-latency object availability

title: Ontology Manager - Overview - Palantir
summary: Ontology Manager (OMA) is the application for building and maintaining the organization's Ontology. Provides a guided step-by-step helper for creating object types with metadata, backing datasource, property mappings, and keys. Columns from existing Foundry datasources are mapped automatically.
relevantParts: Primary UI for Ontology construction and maintenance
Guided object type creation workflow
Automatic column-to-property mapping from datasources

title: Object and Link Types - Object Types - Overview - Palantir
summary: An object type is a schema definition of a real-world entity or event comprised of individual objects. The Foundry Ontology maps each ontological concept to an organization's actual data, enabling this data asset to power real-world applications. Property definitions are analogous to dataset columns.
relevantParts: Object type as schema definition mapping to real data
Property-to-column mapping analogy
Foundation for application-powering data assets

title: Object and Link Types - Properties - Overview - Palantir
summary: Documents the property system in the Ontology, where properties map to columns in backing datasets. Covers property types, required properties, mandatory control properties, and how property values relate to dataset fields.
relevantParts: Property-to-column mapping mechanics
Property types and constraints
Mandatory control properties for access control

title: Data Connection - Exports - Overview - Palantir
summary: Data Connection supports exporting datasets and streams from Foundry to external systems including data warehouses and data lakes. Supports table exports (INSERT/API calls), streaming exports, and file exports, with snapshot and incremental export modes.
relevantParts: Bidirectional data flow: export from Foundry to external systems
Table, streaming, and file export types
Snapshot vs incremental export modes

title: Object Edits and Materializations - Materializations - Palantir
summary: Materializations (formerly writeback datasets in OSv1) capture user edits made through Actions back into persistent Foundry datasets. In OSv2, materializations are optional. Supports automatic propagation of user edits with a few-minutes latency.
relevantParts: Materialization as the bridge between Ontology edits and datasets
Optional in OSv2 vs required in OSv1
Automatic edit propagation capability

title: Object Edits and Materializations - How User Edits Are Applied - Palantir
summary: When an Action is applied to an object, the data-modification logic is immediately applied to the index in object databases and periodically flushed into persistent store as Foundry datasets. Explains the lifecycle of user edits from Action execution through to dataset persistence.
relevantParts: User edit lifecycle from Action to persistent dataset
Immediate index update with periodic dataset flush
Integration between Actions service and object databases

title: Object Permissioning - Multi-datasource Object Types (MDOs) - Palantir
summary: Multi-datasource object types (MDOs) are backed by multiple datasources in the Ontology, supporting column/property-level permissions. Only available in OSv2. Each property must come from exactly one datasource, except the primary key which must exist in every datasource to enable joining.
relevantParts: Multiple datasources backing a single object type
Column-level security through datasource separation
Primary key as the join mechanism across datasources

title: Concepts - Restricted Views - Palantir
summary: Restricted views limit dataset access to only the rows a user has permission to see, built on top of backing datasets. Uses granular policies combining policy definitions with user attributes and group memberships to determine row-level access dynamically.
relevantParts: Row-level security through restricted views
Dynamic policy-based access control
Projection management for read performance

title: Concepts - Markings - Palantir
summary: Markings are Foundry's mandatory access controls allowing data protection officers to centrally manage who can access categories of data. Access is binary (all-or-nothing). Common use cases include PII restriction. Regardless of role, users cannot access data without satisfying all marking requirements.
relevantParts: Mandatory access controls independent of roles
Central data protection management
PII and sensitive data restriction patterns

title: Foundry Architecture - Palantir Learn
summary: Palantir Learn course covering Foundry's overall architecture, providing educational content about how the platform's components work together from data integration through to operational applications.
relevantParts: Educational overview of Foundry architecture
Component interaction patterns

title: Architecture Center - Interoperability - Palantir
summary: Documents Foundry's interoperability capabilities. All data is stored in original format (CSV, Iceberg, Parquet) and accessible through standard interfaces (REST, JDBC, S3-compatible). All transformed data is accessible in open formats like Apache Iceberg and Apache Parquet by default.
relevantParts: Open format data storage (Iceberg, Parquet)
Standard access interfaces (REST, JDBC, S3)
Bidirectional interoperability design

title: Palantir Data Integration Solutions
summary: Palantir's marketing page for Foundry data integration, highlighting the platform's ability to connect data from 200+ sources. Covers the extensible data connection framework, Pipeline Builder, and the full data integration lifecycle from ingestion to Ontology.
relevantParts: 200+ source connectors overview
End-to-end data integration lifecycle
Marketing-level architecture summary

title: Resource Guides - Streaming - Palantir
summary: Resource guide for streaming data integration in Foundry, covering architecture decisions, best practices, and patterns for implementing streaming data pipelines from ingestion through to Ontology availability.
relevantParts: Best practices for streaming data architecture
End-to-end streaming pipeline patterns

title: Palantir Foundry connector overview - Microsoft Fabric
summary: Microsoft's documentation for the Palantir Foundry connector in Microsoft Fabric Data Factory, showing how Foundry integrates with the broader Microsoft data ecosystem for bidirectional data exchange.
relevantParts: Third-party integration with Microsoft Fabric
Enterprise data platform interoperability

title: Palantir Foundry Is 5-10 Years Ahead of Every Other Data Platform - Data Engineer Things
summary: Third-party analysis arguing that Palantir Foundry is significantly ahead of other data platforms, covering pipeline management capabilities that combine change management, data quality, and data loading features.
relevantParts: Independent assessment of Foundry's data engineering capabilities
Pipeline management combining change management and data quality

title: Code Repositories - Overview - Palantir
summary: Code Repositories support authoring data transformation logic in Python, Java, and SQL with features for previewing, debugging, and testing transforms. Supports multi-language repositories, local development, and impact analysis of code changes on downstream datasets.
relevantParts: Multi-language code-based transformation authoring
Impact analysis for code change management
Local development and debugging support

title: Data Connection - Syncs - Set up a streaming sync - Palantir
summary: Documentation for setting up streaming syncs in Data Connection to pull records from external streaming platforms into Foundry streaming datasets with low latency and high throughput.
relevantParts: Streaming sync configuration for real-time data ingestion
Low-latency streaming platform integration

title: Core Concepts - Views - Palantir
summary: Documents views in Foundry's data integration layer, which provide derived or filtered perspectives on underlying datasets without duplicating data, supporting data projection and access patterns.
relevantParts: View abstraction over datasets
Data projection without duplication

title: Action Types - Overview - Palantir
summary: Actions are structured transactions that change objects, properties, and links in the Ontology based on user-defined logic. The Actions service applies user edits to object databases, providing a way to modify property values with complex permissions and conditions.
relevantParts: Actions as the write mechanism for Ontology data
Structured transactions with permission controls
Integration with object databases for data modification

title: Geotemporal Series - Data Modeling - Palantir
summary: Data modeling guidance for geotemporal series in Foundry, covering how to model position changes over time for moving entities. Distinguishes between live properties (change on every observation) and static properties (placed on the object type rather than the series).
relevantParts: Specialized data modeling for geospatial/temporal data
Live vs static property patterns for time-series data

title: Data Catalog - Palantir Foundry
summary: The Data Catalog is an interactive view of curated data and resources in Foundry, organized into Collections and Files. Collections group curated data by topic, audience, or purpose. Access is governed by existing permissions, with purple checkmark badges indicating catalogued data throughout Foundry.
relevantParts: Curated data discovery and organization
Collection-based data categorization
Permission-governed data catalog access

title: Foundry Technical Overview v4 (PDF) - Palantir
summary: Official Palantir technical overview PDF for Foundry, providing a comprehensive architectural description of the platform including data integration, transformation, Ontology, and operational capabilities.
relevantParts: Comprehensive official technical overview document
Full platform architecture description

title: Guidance for Industrial Data Fabric with Palantir Foundry on AWS
summary: AWS guidance document describing how Palantir Foundry operates as an industrial data fabric on AWS infrastructure, covering deployment architecture, data integration patterns, and cloud-native capabilities.
relevantParts: Cloud deployment architecture on AWS
Industrial data fabric integration patterns

title: Over 150 new sources are now available in Data Connection - Palantir Developer Community
summary: Palantir Developer Community announcement of 150+ new supported source types in Data Connection, including DynamoDB, Dataverse, CosmosDB, and LDAP, significantly expanding the platform's data integration capabilities.
relevantParts: 150+ new connector types for enterprise data sources
DynamoDB, Dataverse, CosmosDB, LDAP support

title: Overview and Getting Started - Breaking Changes Between OSv1 and OSv2 - Palantir
summary: Documents the breaking changes between Object Storage V1 (Phonograph) and V2, including the rename of writeback datasets to materializations, materializations becoming optional, and new incremental indexing capabilities. Critical migration reference for understanding Ontology data storage evolution.
relevantParts: OSv1 to OSv2 migration breaking changes
Writeback datasets renamed to materializations
Architectural improvements in OSv2

title: Data Connection - Agents - Agent Worker Configuration Reference - Palantir
summary: Configuration reference for agent workers, which are downloadable programs installed within organizational networks. Agent workers poll Foundry via unidirectional HTTPS connections, execute tasks locally, and push results back. Credentials are encrypted with the agent's public key.
relevantParts: Agent worker deployment and configuration
Unidirectional HTTPS security model
Public key credential encryption

title: Iceberg Tables - Managed vs Virtual Iceberg Tables - Palantir
summary: Compares managed Iceberg tables (managed by Foundry's Iceberg catalog) with virtual Iceberg tables (using external storage and catalogs like Unity, Glue, or Polaris). Covers tradeoffs between data locality and external system integration.
relevantParts: Managed vs virtual Iceberg table architecture comparison
Third-party catalog support (Unity, Glue, Polaris)

title: Overview - Model Integration - Palantir
summary: Covers model integration in Foundry, where models are artifacts encapsulating machine learning, forecasting, optimization, physical models, or business rules. Models can be integrated into data pipelines and the Ontology for operational use.
relevantParts: ML model integration into data pipelines
Models as first-class data pipeline artifacts

title: Foundry Rules - Core Concepts - Object Model - Palantir
summary: Documents the object model used in Foundry Rules, showing how Ontology object types and their relationships are used to drive automated business logic and rules execution within data pipelines.
relevantParts: Object model for automated business rules
Ontology integration with rules engine
